rulings.Case Title: JOHNNIE L. BROWN VS. DENNIS J. HERRERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Strike Pltfs 4th Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 3, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff's 4th Amended Complaint. Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to file a fifth amended complaint that pleads a single cause of action for dangerous condition of public property. Failure to comply with this order will result in dismissal of the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 3, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW LOPEZ's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Granted. If the tentative ruling is contested, Defendant Jung must provide a courtesy copy of his opposition papers to Department 302 no later than 9:00 a.m. on January 3, 2013. If possible, an electronic copy should be sent to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EDWIN ENGBERG et al VS. AKAL SECURITY, INC., A NEW MEXICO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motin And Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, Motion For Protective Order; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 8, PLAINTIFFS EDWIN ENGBERG, MIKE MANIBUSAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This matter was heard on December 28, 2012 by Judge Pro Tem Watson. The parties were informed at the hearing that a ruling on the motion would be withheld until January 3rd to permit the parties to continue to meet and confer. The parties are to inform the court whether issues in the motion have been resolved, by responding via email to contestdept302@sftc.org and jamesw@skwsf.com. In the event any party responds that issues remain unresolved, Judge Pro Tem Watson will issue a ruling. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: HBC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. LUCE FORWARD HAMILTON SCRIPPS, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Furtehr Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Admissions, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, PLAINTIFF HBC ASSOCIATES LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Set One, Request For Admissions, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their positions on (1) whether it would have been reasonable, taking into account the relevant circumstances identified in Sky Valley Ltd. Partnership v. ATX Sky Valley, 150 F.R.D. 648, 651 (N.D. Cal. 1993), for HBC Associates' principal to infer that HBC Associates was, in fact a "client" of Luce Forward; and (2) what effect, if any, arbitrator Quinby's December 21, 2012 ruling denying HBC's motion has on the outcome of HBC Associates' motion to compel in this matter. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: YASIN A. SALMA (AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE YASIN A. AND et al VS. CINDY DUGHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery And In Support Of Pltf'S Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 3, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 7, PLAINTIFFS YASIN A. SALMA (AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE YASIN A. AND MARY J. SALMA 2003 REVOCABLE TRUST), MARY J. SALMA (AS CO TRUSTEE OF THE YASIN A. AND MARY J. SALMA 2003 REVOCABLE TRUST), RYAN SALMA'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery And In Support Of Plaintiff's Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED with respect to Form Interrogatory 15.1, Defendant's responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 with respect to Requests for Admissions10 through 14, 24 through 29, and Defendant's response to Form Interrogatory 50.1; GRANTED with respect to Special Interrogatories 3, 16 through 18, and 25, 27 & 28; GRANTED with respect to Requests for Admission 11, 12, 13, 20 & 21; GRANTED with respect to Demands for Production 4, 13, 14 & 21. Request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
